The automated bed and breakfast service which allows clients to move shares from their dealing accounts to their ISA accounts has been suspended until further notice.The excuse is that many of HL staff are home working so I was advised to sell and reinvest the proceeds in.my ISA account.This suggestion misses the point of a bed and breakfast transaction which is to maintain ownership of the share and  avoid  CGT liability. I question  also why staff are being allowed to be less productive as a result of home working.This is another example of poor customer service or rather a lack of it on HL' s part whilst charging more than any of its rivals.

I ordered 3 rubber hot water bottles from a seller on [BRAND] Marketplace, an order that was fulfilled by [BRAND] ('fulfilled by [BRAND]' just means that the seller stores their goods in one of [BRAND]'s warehouses, and [BRAND] is responsible for sending out the order and arranging any returns). Instead of rubber, I was sent 3 thermoplastic hot water bottles instead, which I am not happy about - I don't buy plastic bottles. I tried to initiate a returns process on the same day the bottles were delivered, only for the website to tell me that this order is not eligible for a return (no reason given). I tried contacting [BRAND], but they are routing all contacts through their chatbot at the moment, and the only reply that keeps giving is that this order is no longer eligible for a return - as if I was tardy in applying for one - even though it was the same day the order was delivered. I have tried to contact [BRAND] about 6 or 7 times, with no success. I know because of being a law graduate that [BRAND] is breaking consumer protection legislation in this country by refusing to provide me with a remedy - they have sent me the wrong goods, goods that are other than described - and now they are refusing to allow me to return the incorrect items: no return, no refund - no refund, no remedy - this is illegal - and at the moment, it seems I have no way of getting through to them to let them know. My health is disabled with several chronic illnesses that leave me unable to work, and although this order wasn't costly I can't afford to waste money on incorrect items that [BRAND] is refusing to allow me to return. Also, the hot water bottles were purchased to help me with my health (one of the chronic illnesses is extremely painful, and the warmth from the bottles helps with the pain). Unfortunately, I am on my last rubber bottle at the moment and it looks like I am going to have to go elsewhere to purchase a spare bottle to use when this one gives out (I use them 24 hours a day, every day of the year, so they don't always last long). I would caution anyone against ordering from [BRAND] at the moment - unless it is from a seller on [BRAND] Marketplace that fulfils their own orders - as they don't seem in the slightest bit interested in providing any customer service whatsoever - so if you have any problem with your order, you may not receive a remedy - and I have found it impossible to get through to their customer service department (it's usually not easy, but doesn't seem in any way feasible at the moment). Caveat emptor - buyer beware!

I tried to initiate a returns process on the same day the bottles were delivered, only for the website to tell me that this order is not eligible for a return (no reason given). I tried contacting [BRAND], but they are routing all contacts through their chatbot at the moment, and the only reply that keeps giving is that this order is no longer eligible for a return - as if I was tardy in applying for one - even though it was the same day the order was delivered. I have tried to contact [BRAND] about 6 or 7 times, with no success. I know because of being a law graduate that [BRAND] is breaking consumer protection legislation in this country by refusing to provide me with a remedy - they have sent me the wrong goods, goods that are other than described - and now they are refusing to allow me to return the incorrect items: no return, no refund - no refund, no remedy - this is illegal - and at the moment, it seems I have no way of getting through to them to let them know. My health is disabled with several chronic illnesses that leave me unable to work, and although this order wasn't costly I can't afford to waste money on incorrect items that [BRAND] is refusing to allow me to return. Also, the hot water bottles were purchased to help me with my health (one of the chronic illnesses is extremely painful, and the warmth from the bottles helps with the pain). Unfortunately, I am on my last rubber bottle at the moment and it looks like I am going to have to go elsewhere to purchase a spare bottle to use when this one gives out (I use them 24 hours a day, every day of the year, so they don't always last long). I would caution anyone against ordering from [BRAND] at the moment - unless it is from a seller on [BRAND] Marketplace that fulfils their own orders - as they don't seem in the slightest bit interested in providing any customer service whatsoever - so if you have any problem with your order, you may not receive a remedy - and I have found it impossible to get through to their customer service department (it's usually not easy, but doesn't seem in any way feasible at the moment). Caveat emptor - buyer beware!

I did the insurance Inside this website. I founded a good price but unfortunately the insurance companies reduced the price but grow up the fees if you leave them before the expire date. Brightside is one of this. If you used the monthly payments and you want leave them before the expire date (for example if you sold the car) they will be apply full amount of fees plus another £60 and they deducted the last amount (£60) from the full amount. In few words a FRAUD. That is a example that you found in the contract:  “If you decide to cancel your insurance policy outside of the cooling off period, the insurer will reclaim a proportion of commission back from us. We will apply a fee equivalent to that amount and deduct it from any returned premium from the insurer, or add it to any outstanding balance due on your premium finance.”  This is what I signed and unfortunately I will be pay. Read carefully before signing.

Very easy to set up, my LISA transfer from another provider was a bit slow but otherwise painless.  I also opened a S&S ISA with no knowledge of the stock market, very easy and simple and you can select your risk level out of 3 levels (although I can't remember what they were called).  you can see your savings grow and each account has a breakdown.   Only gripe is that you can't wire money to invest/save, you have to manually input how much you want to save in the app (although you can set up auto/monthly payments) and it is taken via direct debit and takes approx 1-2 weeks from the point you add the money on the app before it appears in your balance, only issue with this is that if you do this say 1 week before payday, the amount won't come out until after payday so can mess a bit with finances.    The payday boost is great as you can set a monthly amount to invest/save, only issue with this is that you can only set this up for 1 account therefore I have to manually add money into my other ISA.   Overall a great app but it would be nice if you can wire money so it appears instantly rather than wait 1-2 weeks for it to process.

I decided to use it purely due to an advertisment for [BRAND] fuel, I'm autistic so I follow what's Infront of me and expect what I read to be as simple as what I read.  It stated ¬£20 fuel voucher when you buy insurance, OK - I bought insurance and after a while no email or details.  My P.A (who helps me with decisions and dealing with companies) asked me if I received the voucher, I went and looked and only after pressing 'read more' did it open up a step by step.  If this had been clear and not hidden, I would have redeemed it.. not to mention you need an account to purchase insurance through this company so verification could and should be immediate upon a successful purchase, the company clearly knows this is a mistake people will make in order to legally avoid providing something offered to sweeten a deal.  On top of this the information they provide on the insurers is terrible, for the company I went with there was a lot of hidden fees regarding changes and cancellations charged not just by the broker, but both companies... this information is only provided AFTER purchasing the insurance.  I've never posted a review before, but I feel this company exploited my disability in order to sell me a low quality service and lured me in on a dishonest redemption policy.  I realise people who can be forward and confrontational/confident with companies can have much better experiences, but I hope anyone reading this can realise others don't have this ability, and get exploited regularly in different ways by companies like confused.  Worst part was when they refused me, they're still actually running the promotion as of the date I posted this.

[BRAND] don‚Äôt check the quality of the insurance policies they recommend.  After an internet search in Aug 2019 on [BRAND], we took out a single event travel insurance policy with [BRAND].  A week later in September 2019, we travelled to the Airport and was in the departure lounge before hearing [BRAND] had cancelled our flight. Thinking we‚Äôd be covered by our insurance, we made alternate arrangements with the airline (alt airport, alt date), this arrangement did however occur some minor costs.  On our return we contacted [BRAND] recommended [BRAND] with a claim for our occurred costs i.e. extra taxi‚Äôs, loss of transfers, purchase of new transfers and loss of night accommodation, total ¬£267. Only to get it bounced almost immediately via a email, after complaining they did but our claim up for processing via another company (travel insurance services).  After several unsuccessful attempts for updates from both companies, we got a call from a withheld number asking for confirmation of bank details. The caller refused to offer any other details other than she was from [BRAND]/travelinsuranceservices (I forget which), I in-turn refused to share my bank details on the grounds we don‚Äôt share such things with unsolicited callers and that even if genuine I didn‚Äôt want an unagreed amount of money dropping into my account and that being the close of the matter. I then got a series of emails asking for the same, which I again resisted for the latter reason. Then they assured me that no money would be paid without consultation, so I shared my details. For the next two weeks I got several requests for my bank details, despite them being previously shared and being part of the now very long email trail.  Two weeks later I again chased progress, only to be informed a Settlement agreement had been paid ten days previously. Exactly what I was worried about and was assured would happen. I then got a email confirming the settlement agreement and methods of appeal. Appeal I did and was rejected, stating the amount paid was correct, to their policy. Which I‚Äôve since read and found to be correct.   WHAT is the point of taking out UPGRADED travel insurance, if it doesn‚Äôt cover a mere ¬£267 of costs occurred when your flight is CANCELLED!!! They paid me 22% of this and made every step difficult, with their unprofessional and aloof behaviour. I hope nobody has a real holiday disaster, after taking a [BRAND] recommendation and insuring themselves with [BRAND]!  BTW, [BRAND] claimed the cancellation was due to circumstances out of their control and refused to consider compensation. So I lost a day of my holiday, lost my transfer, plus I was out of pocket and NOBODY put their hands up, said sorry or paid me what we lost!!!

Whilst [BRAND] are an interesting tool, the information input has to be spot on to get a reliable comparison. The savings purported to be made are based on the wrong set of tariffs They are not like for like (they seem to be compared with a standard tariff) Even quotes from your existing supplier use the same tactics. For example, I was quoted a saving of £104.33 by switching supplier, but when the breakdowns are examined and whilst standing charges may be less or unit rates is less, the overall saving was NOT a saving at all If I switched I would be paying an EXTRA £20.06 a year So, do your own homework with a calculator, pen and paper before you commit All is not that it might seem

I can't say [BRAND] are my favourite company, but there is often little choice but to use them, particularly during the current lock-down. The site generally works well, with a returns policy which ordinarily functions well. After receiving so many sub-standard Chinese-made items, which take months to arrive, and bear little resemblance to what is described, I take many reviews with a pinch of salt, and think [BRAND] could do more to penalise rogue traders. Price fluctuation is also often huge. Items are often ridiculously over-packaged upon arrival. Deliveries are not always reliable, particularly at the moment, and I particularly resent waiting in all day, only to be informed that I have not been in. Their delivery drivers are undoubtedly underpaid and rushed off their feet, but it's hard to sympathise on the occassions when they blatantly lie.

The site generally works well, with a returns policy which ordinarily functions well. After receiving so many sub-standard Chinese-made items, which take months to arrive, and bear little resemblance to what is described, I take many reviews with a pinch of salt, and think [BRAND] could do more to penalise rogue traders. Price fluctuation is also often huge. Items are often ridiculously over-packaged upon arrival. Deliveries are not always reliable, particularly at the moment, and I particularly resent waiting in all day, only to be informed that I have not been in. Their delivery drivers are undoubtedly underpaid and rushed off their feet, but it's hard to sympathise on the occassions when they blatantly lie.

I have been a [BRAND] member for close to 10 years and have had very few problems. Normally, I would highly recommend paying for it. However, I ordered an iPad on April 30 for my mother, who needs it to communicate with me during the pandemic. It was supposed to arrive in two days. I waited five days and checked the Website, where I read a message that said the iPad was running late, but would be arriving two days later. It didn't. Instead, I read another message with the date moved two days further out. After working my way through layers of bots and Q and A's, I finally found a human who assured me it was on its way. On May 10, I again worked my way through the bots and Q and A's to a human, who told me it had been lost in shipping. Thanks for that. In the meantime, all of my other options for buying the iPad had sold out. I'll think twice before ordering in-demand electronics from [BRAND].

I want to say, everytime I purchase something that is "fulfilled' by [BRAND]; I have an issue. It is always the driver delivering it to the wrong address or not following the delivery instructions I provide.  When it happens, customer service is usually good about it. Not this time.  I received notice that my item was next to be delivered. About 10 minutes later, I received notice that it was delivered. The picture showed a rug outside the door it was left by. I do not have a rig by my door.   I tried to call customer service and was told no one is answering due to the pandemic. I then got online and started chatting with a customer service rep. This was all within 5 minutes of the delivery notice. I asked them to have the driver come back, get the package and deliver it to the right address. The rep said it was scanned and marked as delivered by accident. I said, I suppose the app telling me I am the next stop, the package being placed in front of a door, the order marked as delivered and a picture being taken is all an accident as well? The rep said it was. Again, I asked for the driver to come back and deliver the package to me. The rep asked me to wait until tomorrow for delivery, despite me telling her several times it was already delivered.   She continued to argue until I finally asked for my money back. My chat was transferred 2 times to a different rep. The 3rd one finally said he issued a refund. We shall see.

I use [BRAND] Home Delivery at least once a week. I always find the delivery drivers very helpful and polite. I only have three comments-  on the app, the meal deals where you choose a combination of 2 or 3 things should have the items in groups as at present you have to scroll through everything to find all the different mains, sides, sweets, etc. When you buy unwrapped fruit such as mangoes, avocados, pineapples the best before dates have been removed. Lastly, if I choose a delivery between 8 and 9pm or onwards and have an item with a use by date of 2 days, surely that day should not be included as it’s unlikely I am going to use that item on the first day as it arrives so late in the evening then the item has only one day before being past by its sell by date.  The convenience of being able to buy my food shopping from in my own home and having it delivered straight to my kitchen table is great. If you have a budget to stick to you know exactly what you’re spending.

I’m Christmas shopping, so I have a lot to buy, but after a half hour of shopping on this app, it automatically empty’s you’re basket so there’s no time to browse and pick up some extras that you wasn’t initially looking for. Unless you order a few items at a time which would mean multiple delivery charges. It’s absolutely infuriating when you spend time adding things to your basket and Broussard’s g for it all to be emptied and you have to start all over again. For this reason, I only shop online with [BRAND] if it’s a must. With all other shopping apps, I add things to my basket as I remember about them then checkout when I’ve got everything. With this app I have to jot things down and try and blast through and find everything within the allocated time, it’s like a supermarket sweep. Also during checkout, on the upside I had some vouchers through my credit card, to apply and they went through no problem at all, but then it wouldn’t let me checkout for free delivery despite spending about £500, it would only let me “pay” when I tapped on the £3.[BRAND] delivery option. It’s little things like this that make this app so user unfriendly.  I shop online all the time, I don’t go to shops, so as I go about my days, I like to drop things in my basket as I remember about them. I presume that the reason for the basket emptying is to make people checkout fast, but in my case anyway it totally backfired on [BRAND] because,  for 1, it makes it a thought to use the app in the first place,  2, I have to paper and pencil a list and type and shop at a million miles an hour to get everything on one order unless I want to do lots of smaller orders and incur lots delivery charges.  I love [BRAND] in the actual shops, very good customer service, but this app is just a headache for me. It’s a real shame.   Everything [BRAND] sells is of such high standard and along with the good customer service and very reasonable pricing, it’s just a real, big shame this lets them down so badly.   Sort this [BRAND]!!

As I drive a very expensive performance car I expect to pay a high premium and thought I might have difficulty getting quotes on [BRAND]. I nearly didn't bother but faced with an annual bill of £2679 I gave it a whirl. It took me about 10 minutes to fill in the form and about another 5 for quotes to arrive. I took immediate cover with a national 'name' for £699. My documents were with me in minutes. So about half an hour start to finish. Big Bonus.....£1980 better off. Sounds like a fairy tale but absolutely true. I also own an expensive pick up truck. Renewal due in a month. My current policy is £783 before any increase at renewal. I ran this through [BRAND] and now have a quote for £280. Another £500 saved. Best hours work I have done in a long time We have 2 other cars in the household and will obviously be back.

I've been with this platform for years when it was TDW but most recently as iii. I was reasonably happy with the service until I came to transfer my ISA out to another provider. The process is slow and I am being told it could take up to 5 weeks to transfer the cash value (approx. £180k). I have another ISA with Share Centre (which they now own) and that has been fairly swift. I requested at the same time. Smacks of iii hanging on to the money deliberately. I have sent two emails now asking why this is taking so long but no replies from iii.   Also if I want to close my account I only get access to my online documents for 6 weeks. This is appalling. The access to these important documents needed for tax purposes etc should be allowed well in excess of this. This again is also possible on other platforms. I am not likely to be coming back to iii. Not impressed.

I WISH THE SYSTEM WOULD ALLOW ME TO GIVE THIS ORGANISATION MINUS POINTS  We have just started on our new contract with 247 home rescue for gas services and landlord certificates. This service is prominently publicised by [BRAND] . The contract was very easy to set up. Then we were asked to do some (unnecessary) work at significant expense as part of the service, which we declined.  We were also asked to pay extra for bringing forward our services to fit in with our landlord certificate. it was very difficult to persuade them to have appointments within 12 months of the previous certificate. They finally found times, and duly charged us for bringing the dates forward.  The day of the services arrived. They cancelled on the day. They gave us new times on the following working day.  Then, a few hours later, the plumber arrived when the tenants had left!  Today, they phoned to say the services would take place in the morning. Then, at 11.30, they were cancelled again.  This is totally hopeless.  We would strongly urge anyone considering a landlord contract with 247 Home Service to look elsewhere. Even [BRAND], with all its faults, is infinitely more reliable.   Why [BRAND] is sponsoring this service is beyond our understanding. its reputation has suffered considerably by association in our eyes.

Watch out if you go to [BRAND] for house insurance - CHECK THE COVER OFFERED BY THE COMPANIES THEY RECOMMEND BEFORE YOU BUY.  I clearly stated that my house had been flooded and as such wanted the insurance to cover this risk. [BRAND] listed quotes from several companies and I chose the cheapest - but due to difficulties in connection contacted them by phone and gave the quote number. I have almost completed buying the insurance when the person I was speaking to casually mentioned that they didn't cover against flooding. When I pointed out that this was listed in the details I provided they simply stated that they couldn't provide the cover. When I went back to [BRAND] to complain and get an explanation all I got was platitudes and the comment that it was entirely up to myself to check the scope of the cover - all they did was provide comparison and were not responsible for the level of cover. Funny though - when I went back a few days later to check if the same company was being recommended I found it was ...... but this time it carried a statement that it didn't cover for flooding EVEN IF YOU HAD ASKED FOR IT IN THE DETAILS PROVIDED

Unfortunately this is another one of those companies that are happy to take their cut of your money but offer no consumer protection if something goes wrong.  I recently booked car hire using their subsidiary website Travel Supermarket.  Unfortunately the agent at the end of the chain - [BRAND] - failed to deliver the goods I had paid for - i.e. a hire car at Dublin airport.  This caused stress and upset for my family.    As the introducer I raised a complaint with [BRAND] where they basically pass the buck along the chain, even including the consumer, as it was my informed choice to use the chosen broker.    Whilst I accept that [BRAND] were not responsible for the issue they should be vetting the organisations they promote on their site to protect us as consumers.  Another disappointing company - great if everything goes right but offer no protection if things fail.

Thank you!

I was most reluctant to change to DD, after years of making quarterly cash payments with [BRAND] without incidence or any late payments. However, the only saving tariffs SP offers are to customers on DD. This means that SP is effectively excluding many people from accessing cheaper tariff rates. Amongst these are elderly, disabled, people as well as younger people or families on low incomes, or even those who simply do not wish to use this online service. Furthermore, within the documents explaining the new tariffs there is no mention of this DD exclusivity. Shame on them! They claim that this is not discrimination, but is very helpful to customers. Their reasoning does not make up for freedom of choice.  The [BRAND] agent who helped me patiently explore the possibilities also emcouraged me to eventually consider trying DD, which I have done.  Thank you!

This is the canary in the coal mine phenomenon. If we can't trust [BRAND] then what is there left to trust in corporate Britain.  I recently chose a broadband company based on their recommendation. The promised cashback never arrived. I have emailed [BRAND] and have had no response. All I need is a copy of the recommendation article so I can prove that the cashback is due.   I don't trust them anymore. And since trust is what their business model is based on I can't see how this company can remain as lucrative a money earner in the long term.  A reputation takes years to build and a day to lose. They should be taking this seriously.  UPDATE: I SENT TWO EMAILS TO [BRAND] 6 AND 8 DAYS AGO RESPECTIVELY (INCLUDING TO THE EMAIL YOU PROVIDED IN YOUR RESPONSE) AND HAVE HAD NO MORE THAN AN AUTOMATED RESPONSE. DO YOU REALLY CONSIDER THIS GOOD ENOUGH?  DO YOU HAVE ANY DATA WITH REGARDS THE NUMBER OF EMAILS YOU RECEIVE MONTHLY FROM DISGRUNTLED CUSTOMERS ABOUT CASH BACKS AND VOUCHERS THAT NEVER MATERIALISE. AND TO THE PERCENTAGE OF COMPLAINT EMAILS YOU EVEN RESPOND TO. AND THE PERCENTAGE OF SUCCESSFUL OUTCOMES TO CUSTOMERS FOLLOWING COMPLAINT. I WOULD LIKE TO SEE FULL TRANSPARENCY FROM YOU ON THIS IMPORTANT MATTER. (ALTHOUGH I KNOW BETTER THAN TO EXPECT THAT YOU EVEN HAVE SUFFICIENT RECORDS TO ANSWER THE ABOVE, OR WOULD WISH TO SHARE THIS INFORMATION IF YOU DID HAVE THE RECORDS).  Edit on 20 March 2019  This exchange with [BRAND] is ludicrous. I am apparently always just one step away from a response, just send your email to this email instead they say. Then when there is no response it's because some very particular wording was not stated in the subject line.  Let's be clear here about the issue. This company promotes other company's services and expects us to trust them enough to spend our hard-earned cash at their recommendation. Yet, when it is provided with information that clearly points to customers being scammed or misked it does not even care to respond. We are talking here about a company that has revenues in the hundreds of millions of pounds (which they have profited from our trust) and they apparently won't even pay someone to read the emails that concerned customers are sending them.   It would be interesting to know if the company is going to rot since Martin Lewis sold [BRAND] to [BRAND]. I also think there should be an independent investigation into [BRAND] relationships with the companies it promotes. (if it is too cozy then the public needs to be warned). I do hope that this final review does not get a response from [BRAND] as I'm done with this and won't be checking back in to see what further false assurances they make with regards to a response. I know they received all three of my emails as they have sn auto-responder.

Being using them for 20 years in four different countries: all excellent generally.  Quite happy to pay the '[BRAND] Premium' for the convenience.   I have made recommendations as regards service improvements in past (such as being able to sort by price & shipping combine to stop £0.01 goods with £2.50 shipping; put parcel in 'Special Place behind the rubbish bin') and then noticed them change policy; I'm sure others suggested the same. When things go wrong - and they do over that time period due to sheer volume - they have done what was needed as quick as I expected. A minor point, hence 4 / 5 stars, is that recently they have let some products with misleading descriptions be sold by the 3rd party sellers - and seem to be utterly incapable or unwilling to remove them and keep them removed despite multiple reports.  True, the external vendors are being deliberately misleading / deceitful....but [BRAND] runs the site, they take the commission - so it is their responsibility to clean up their 'house'; the ASA agreed.

Since changing the rules (for the third time in six months) I can no longer sell shares if the value is below £2. So if I hold a single share below £2 it can’t be sold.   I have been asking them to sell the shares using the in messaging system and no one will authorize but the software won’t let me sell.   The charts don’t load properly and take a few minutes to fix themselves. Which has given me a heart attack more than once.   The ISA wrapper is a little pricy and if you do intend to hold on long term to the shares that is a excellent way to protect your profits. Here it costs £36 a year. You can get it for free on other platforms.   I think there are better value platforms and the fact that I am meant to hold my shares in a company that requires frequent crowdfunding to keep afloat is unsettling to me.

I’m used to navigating the [BRAND] site now but in the beginning I struggled. If you persevere you will soon find it’s very easy. Make sure money is in the right account by midnight as they take payment soon after midnight. Look for email in morning if you want to know substitutions bcos not all drivers tell you about them so it can come as quite a surprise when you are putting it away and not always a good surprise. Having mentioned these minor issues I want to stress that overall it is easy once you get used to it. Good value for money, very reasonably priced and most of the delivery guys go way over and beyond what is expected- I am disabled and unable to unpack boxes of shopping quickly so the guys are usually extremely good and unpack the boxes for me.i am so very appreciative of what they do, always with a smile too. A couple of them put fridge stuff away for me too as it’s directly opposite front door and that small act of kindness has made the whole delivery process a lot easier for me I’m forever grateful for this. Thank-you xx Sarah x

Went to [BRAND] to look for a new energy supplier, and it presents sensible questions on address, fuel and annual usage. From this, the theory is that all energy suppliers and tariffs are checked and presented to you. We were offered several deals, saving us up to £500 yearly. However, we almost failed to notice a little checkbox saying, "Click here to show tariffs we cannot transfer you to". Even when we noticed it, we guessed that it might be because we do not live in the right area. Being brave, we clicked it and revealed several suppliers that would save us up to £900, and signed up to one of them very easily. It appears that [BRAND] will not recommend suppliers that do not give them a 'cut'. Not the independent or money-saving advice you might be hoping for... Made me worry about those times I had looked up exchange rates or car hire.

Hi well the story i have is four A4 pages so will not fit in here. Suffice to say went through what i thought would be [BRAND] quote and ended up taking 4 hours of my time.  After I bought Car insurance 18 Dec 2018 via [BRAND] I got hit with a form asking for my National Insurance Number. Never been asked that before and does seem Over the Top.  Now on High Alert who would want to give away your National Insurance Number to strangers (thought that was for Government Agencies) had me concerned and did some homework. Seems this question has been asked before maybe since 2014 from what I have read via the internet. Must be behind the times never heard of this myself. Okay still concerned do not want to give away my NI Number to anyone it ties in to my Finance and pension and identity. And what with all the things that i try to protect myself from like dodgy phone calls from persons trying to rip you off by you by saying they are BT and [BRAND] the list goes on. So Yes i am concerned about giving away MY NI number. Tried to get some information out of [BRAND] and that was a disaster they had not heard of the NI number form for Car insurance.  Went to Car Insurance company and asked them about the NI number. They said yes right enough they need this hymn (Still do not want to give away my NI number would rather cancel insurance in my mind) after asking a few more questions I finally got another way to send the NI number this time hidden within a code it is something called a"Check Code". now why was I not told this from [BRAND] I just do not know. It would have saved me 4 hours of my time from waiting on the telephone in every direction (Call Center music gets boring after the first call) should have picked two stars for this as it was inadequate experience with a lot of friction (they were not rude) but down to one star for amount of time i spent looking for answers

I have been using this app for some time now and it has improved from when I first swapped over from shopping through the website. When I first used the app it usually crashed at the payment window. I found this extremely annoying as it required signing in again. This bug seems to have been sorted and I can now shop from signing in to signing out without any problems. So well done there. The whole shop is easy with the favourites listed and if you don’t require any other goods not on your list the whole shop can be done and dusted in a very short time. There is now a wider choice in the non food department, gift wrap, stationery, postage stamps etc another good thing. My only niggle is when you try to remove an item that’s no longer available it shows you’ve removed it but it is still on your list, hence 4 stars.

This app is clearly polished, but it’s evident that it is functionally deficient. Unlike other reward services, [BRAND] wants you to use the app to scan your card, presumably so that it can first bombard you with all the other content in the app. When what most users want is just to store their card in Apple Wallet for easy reference when they need to scan it. Based on their Twitter feed responses it is very clear that [BRAND] don’t care what their customers want and they seem to be adamant in their policy of not supporting Apple Wallet. Even though the app description in the App Store claims support for Apple Wallet. My advice is to shop with stores and loyalty programmes that put the customer rather than their own business priorities first and centre.

These guys misled me into funding my account. I received no less than three emails informing (and congratulating) me that my account application had been 'approved'. On that basis, I transferred a not insignificant sum. The problems started when I went to make my first trade, but received an error message saying that my residential address was still being verified. Surely, if my account was 'approved', that meant that all checks were complete and my account was ready to use? Apparently not with IB! I would not have funded the account had I not been led to think that everything was good to go.   So, on to the customer support, which is really, really, really, slow and unhelpful. After an hour-long live chat with excruciating waits in between responses, I was told that someone would be contacting me by email soon to help me get it resolved ASAP. I accepted that, but, still annoyed, filed my own complaint ticket as well. 24 hours later, I had received neither an email from an IB representative (aside from another generic one reminding me that my account was now 'approved' and funded!), nor a response to my complaint ticket.   Having lost faith in IB and wanting to take my business elsewhere, I tried to withdraw my funds and close my account, only to be told that I must wait three days before I can do so. Given that I was misled into sending the funds in the first place, I think this is absolutely shocking.   IB may be one of the cheaper brokers, but it is clearly a case of you get what you pay for. Avoid them at all costs.

My experience is only around the Parking forum, so my review is based on this specific experience. As someone who needed information on pursuing actions around an unfair parking fine it was pretty good to read, although there are plenty of other very good forums and sites out there too. It’s free so that’s another bonus. But as a normal person, just there looking for information, some discussion as needed and of course a little moral support, I was really shocked by the OTT attitudes and behaviour of some of the regular forum members. Snooty, supercilious, self serving and anti anyone new who’s just finding their way with a specific issue - without the new people who seek info and advice from it, this forum wouldn’t exist would it so why abuse us? I think the administrators need to ‘police’ it better. Just like any forum you come across, it has people there who for them being a regular on it is their life and it comes across that their egos are far more important than the purpose of the forum, which is supposed to be to provide altruistic help to members of the public. I’m afraid I did experience bullying behaviour from ‘regulars’ with repeated attempts to belittle me. There were also some very good people and some good tips but as usual the bad are louder, distract from the reason you’re there and tend to be what you remember.

I recently contacted [BRAND] regarding gas and electric options.  I was advised I could save £159 against my current spend which they stated was £1399.56.  The reduced cost, with an alternative supplier, would therefore be £1240.56.  Sounds good - not really as the annual cost has been grossly inflated.  My annual spend with my current supplier, after 11 months, is actually predicted to be £1137.41 and not, as they say, £1399.56, that is at the best very misleading and at the worst quite deceitful. £1137.41 is considerably less (£262.15) than their figure of £1399.56.  So their quoted savings of £159 is untrue and meaningless.  I have sent 2 e-mails to [BRAND] asking for an explanation but have failed to receive any response.  Their so called predictions and savings are just pie in the [BRAND].  As far as I am concerned there are only 2 factors that determine the best deal and they are, very simply, the cost per Kwh and the daily charge.  That is all that is required to make an accurate comparison,  Malcolm Cohen

Another email followed where he said I should see the new tariff on my [BRAND]  account by the end of today and sure enough it is there.   Thanks Carl and [BRAND], [BRAND] needs to employ people  like you, who know what good customer service is. [BRAND] should also be very grateful to you because I was within a hair’s breadth of cancelling the switch and finding another company., which I would have done through [BRAND]

I’ve been with IB for many years and closed out my account a few months ago. They are just too fragmented as a business and their software has become archaic compared to what is now available. I have only needed to call them once in the time I was with them. Somehow my tax I’d got changed and I was sent a letter that they were going to withhold a major part of my account for the irs. I went to the link they provided in their letter and it was non existent. Long story my issue had to be bumped a few times to get to correct department that could fix the issue.   IB was great when it was pretty much the only game in town, but times have changed and IB hasn’t. What I didn’t like is that they started charging data fees  while other brokers included it. Their software is pieced together and there is much better available from other brokers and third party providers. Now that fees are becoming competitive I switched brokers.   I checked out their lite version with no fees. Apparently this does not include TWS. No thanks.

I love this app, so way to use and I have had none of the problems reported by others. Although not necessary I would suggest that you at least understand the basics of investing before using and sign up to the community. Pay attention to costs and fees that there are surrounding ETF’s, do your own company research and consider other ISA investment platforms if you are a low level user but other than that I was up and running in no time at all. My only issue is no quick link to the community which is a great source of information and a place to while away the time waiting for a result. Certainly not for the gamers, gamblers and Warren Buffets of this world if you want the stress of sweating on a share price, but then if you haven’t assessed your risk levels you shouldn’t really be investing in the first place.

This site provides quote for companies that have no interest in their customers and people who offer low premiums but have no cover and every term and condition to help them get out of paying a claim. Do not trust the insurance companies they provide.   QA Choice Insurance/ XS Direct: This company has caused me nothing but trouble and put me in the worst financial difficulty. I made a claim and the company took 3 weeks to make a decision to void the insurance due to the V5 being in my name (the named driver) instead of my mums name (the main driver). The company refused to give us back our £3800 premium and yet still charged us £3000 charges. This has left us in £24,000 debt and a void on our insurance policy. This company will do everything in its power to not support their customers and get out of paying any claim. AVOID AT ALL TIMES!

To this end T212 and all the other pretty apps  like this need far more educational areas and regulating. You‚Äôre in denial if you think you can rock up and make cash that easily without any blowback. Well this new nhs repeat prescription service online is a step in the right direction, but with so many flaws!  First, it’s is difficult switching between multiple people (I order for my whole family, 4 people). This is dangerous because items can unintentionally be ordered for the wrong person. I am careful so this is unlikely, but the potential is there. To switch, you have to click [BRAND] logo, but this isn’t obvious at all!!  There is no option to change email address, you have to deactivate and start again, losing patient history of ordered meds.  There is no option to add medication on standby in bag to order at a later date. I am just trying to set it up for my family (don’t need ordering now, but later) so now I have to wait to add med details in until it is time to order instead of it being pre-populated.   There is no updates as advertised (request sent to gp, approved by gp, packing meds etc). The only notification I got was order received and order ready to collect. And can’t view this on the service online.  Also when ordering once set up, you have to go through all the details again. Why isn’t there a function to remember basic details like gp practice, chosen pharmacy of collection, exemption status etc. This drags out the process for each person. This is important to add in, there should just be an option to change details if needed.   Otherwise, app looks sleek and again a step in the right direction. Please fix the above flaws that stick out like a sore thumb!!

First, it’s is difficult switching between multiple people (I order for my whole family, 4 people). This is dangerous because items can unintentionally be ordered for the wrong person. I am careful so this is unlikely, but the potential is there. To switch, you have to click [BRAND] logo, but this isn’t obvious at all!!  There is no option to change email address, you have to deactivate and start again, losing patient history of ordered meds.  There is no option to add medication on standby in bag to order at a later date. I am just trying to set it up for my family (don’t need ordering now, but later) so now I have to wait to add med details in until it is time to order instead of it being pre-populated.   There is no updates as advertised (request sent to gp, approved by gp, packing meds etc). The only notification I got was order received and order ready to collect. And can’t view this on the service online.  Also when ordering once set up, you have to go through all the details again. Why isn’t there a function to remember basic details like gp practice, chosen pharmacy of collection, exemption status etc. This drags out the process for each person. This is important to add in, there should just be an option to change details if needed.   Otherwise, app looks sleek and again a step in the right direction. Please fix the above flaws that stick out like a sore thumb!!

[BRAND] customer service has gotten worse. Their drivers have, on several occasions, indicated that a package was delivered, even going so far as to take a picture, only to discover they lied about having made the delivery. I have gone through the very painful process of contacting them, only to get no resolution.   The most recent incident began April 14, 2020. I ordered birthday gifts for my great grandson in Houston area, whose birthday was May 6, 2020. [BRAND] indicated the package (with picture) was delivered on April 17, 2020. I ordered birthday gifts early because I was unsure how fast deliveries could be made during Covid-19. Some items were delivered on 4/17/20, but my granddaughter just told me today that one large package didn't arrive until YESTERDAY, May 6th, on his actual birthday. I discovered this today when I called my granddaughter to ask how Jasai's 2nd birthday went and if he liked his gifts. That's when I was told the driver knocked on her door to give her the package.  You might say, "Well done! It arrived ON his birthday!" However, as I have said to [BRAND] customer service, when the drivers lie about when packages are delivered, it could create a problem if the item was NEVER delivered. It has happened to me recently, with the USPS. [BRAND] indicated that the item was delivered and yet my friend in Chicago has yet to receive the item. Dealing with the postal service is just as infuriating as dealing with [BRAND]'s customer service. Your package never arrives and your are out of the price of your purchases.   [BRAND] seems to go out of their way to make it near impossible to get customer service. I'm writing here because when I attempted to chat with [BRAND]'s customer service, I was switched to something called Just Answer. For the price of a dollar (which they claim is refunded) you then wait for the system to match you with a lawyer (yes, that's right, a lawyer!) who will then call you or email you the answer to your question.  Has anyone any advice on how I can do an end run around the [BRAND] obstacle course. I've tried their chat, they no longer answer their phones, I'm at a loss.

It’s delivered to the door at a time that suits her, it’s been really good for her. Only downside now is there’s no longer an option for carrier bags for the shopping which might not seem much, but the baskets the shopping is delivered in are very heavy even to go from door to kitchen and the bags made it a lot easier, so now we have to get it delivered at a time when the grandkids will be there to help unpack it all to make it easier on her

Please help.
